The Molecular Sciences Institute:
Drew Endy
Drew Endy
Research Fellow

The Molecular Sciences Institute
2168 Shattuck Avenue
Berkeley, California 94704
510.647.0690x130
510.647.0699 (fax)
dendy@molsci.org (PGP public key available here)
Drew Endy earned a B.S. in civil engineering (1992) and a M.S. in enviromental engineering (1994) from Lehigh University, and a Ph.D. degree in biochemical engineering from Dartmouth College (1998). He did a joint postdoc in microbiology/genetics and chemical engineering at the University of Texas-Austin and the University of Wisconsin-Madison, respectively. He joined the Molecular Sciences Institute in October, 1998. His research focuses on the deconstruction and synthesis of biological systems, with a particular emphasis on the development of a quantitative system-level biology. His mantra is "biology is a medium for creation." Current research projects include the design and synthesis of a "simple" biological system, a computational/experimental study of the yeast pheromone signal transduction pathway, and the development of new computational methods for representing large systems of biochemical reactions.

Links

  • The Bacteriophage T7 Page

Publications

  • Drew Endy, Lingchong You, John Yin, Ian Molineux, "Computation, prediction, and experimental tests of fitness for bacteriophage T7 mutants with permuted genomes," PNAS-USA, 97(10),5375-5380. .PDF reprint
  • Drew Endy, John Yin, "Toward Antiviral Strategies that Resist Viral Escape," Antimicrobial Agents and Chemotherapy, 44(4),1097-1099 (2000). .PDF reprint
  • Drew Endy, "Towards a Predictive Biology: The Example of Bacteriophage T7," Proceedings of DIMACS workshop on Evolution as Computation, Jan 11-12, Princeton University, Landweber L and Winfree E, eds., Springer-Verlag, NY, (1999).
  • Drew Endy, "Development and Application of a Genetically-Structured Simulation for Bacteriophage T7," Dissertation, Thayer School of Engineering, Dartmouth College, Hanover NH, 188 pages (1997).
  • Drew Endy, Deyu Kong, and John Yin, "Intracellular Kinetics of a Growing Virus: A Genetically-Structured Simulation for Bacteriophage T7," Biotechnology & Bioengineering, 55(2), 375-389 (1997). .PDF reprint
  • Drew Endy, "Biologically catalyzed reduction of nitrous oxide to nitrogen gas in wastewater treatment systems," Proceedings of the 27th Mid-Atlantic Industrial and Hazardous Waste Conference, 255-263 (1995).

Awards and Honors

  • Office of Naval Research Grant #N00014-99-1-0556 (1999)
  • C.F. & R.D. Goodrich Prize, Thayer School of Engineering, Dartmouth College (1998)
  • H.F. Darling 1926 Fellowship, Thayer School of Engineering, Dartmouth College (1994)
  • A. Humphrey Teaching Award, College of Engineering, Lehigh University (1993)
